name: MÜLLERIAN DUCT CYST
definition: failed regression of paramesonephric (= müllerian) duct which usually regresses by 3rd fetal

demographics.Prevalence: 4-5% of male newborns; in 1% of men
demographics.Location: arises from region of verumontanum slightly lateral to midline
demographics.Associated with: typically NO other congenital anomalies; renal agenesis (rare)

dominant_finding: increased signal on T1WI ← hemorrhage, protein
distribution: arises from region of verumontanum slightly lateral to midline
discriminating_features: large teardrop-shaped intraprostatic cyst usually with extension superolaterally above
aspirate contains serous / mucous clear brown / green fluid (hemorrhage + debris), NOT
increased signal on T1WI ← hemorrhage, protein

general: large teardrop-shaped intraprostatic cyst usually with extension superolaterally above
aspirate contains serous / mucous clear brown / green fluid (hemorrhage + debris), NOT
MR: increased signal on T1WI ← hemorrhage, protein
